title: OPC-UA Message Tampering Indicators via Signature Failures

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for SR 3.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 3.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.27: OPC-UA Message Tampering Indicators via Signature Failures.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.27' running on edge_hub_opcua security headers, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_opcua" earliest=-24h
| eval mode=coalesce(opcua_security_mode,"None")
| stats count by opcua_server_endpoint, mode, opcua_security_policy_uri
| eventstats sum(count) as tot by opcua_server_endpoint
| eval pct=round(100*count/tot,2)
| where mode="None" AND pct>1
| sort - pct
description: Raises alerts when OPC-UA signing/encryption modes downgrade or signature checks fail, indicating integrity issues on industrial sessions.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_protocol and sourcetypes edge_hub_opcua. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Disable None except lab VLANs; (2) baseline each server; (3) alert on new None percentage; (4) correlate with firewall TLS inspection if used. Indexes required: `index=ot_protocol`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=edge_hub_opcua`.
visualization: Stacked bar (mode mix), Table, Single value (servers with None).
knownFalsePositives: Zone communications during authorized engineering, vendor maintenance, or planned commissioning. PAM sessions from authorized engineering access, vendor remote support, or scheduled maintenance.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `edge_hub_opcua` security headers.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Disable None except lab VLANs; (2) baseline each server; (3) alert on new None percentage; (4) correlate with firewall TLS inspection if used.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_opcua" earliest=-24h
| eval mode=coalesce(opcua_security_mode,"None")
| stats count by opcua_server_endpoint, mode, opcua_security_policy_uri
| eventstats sum(count) as tot by opcua_server_endpoint
| eval pct=round(100*count/tot,2)
| where mode="None" AND pct>1
| sort - pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**OPC-UA Message Tampering Indicators via Signature Failures (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 3.1)** — Raises alerts when OPC-UA signing/encryption modes downgrade or signature checks fail, indicating integrity issues on industrial sessions.

Documented **Data sources**: `edge_hub_opcua` security headers. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_protocol; **sourcetype**: edge_hub_opcua. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_protocol, sourcetype="edge_hub_opcua", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **mode** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by opcua_server_endpoint, mode, opcua_security_policy_uri** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by opcua_server_endpoint** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where mode="None" AND pct>1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT engineering record and session recording the control owner would use in an investigation. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar (mode mix), Table, Single value (servers with None).
grandmaExplanation: We raises alerts when OPC-UA signing/encryption modes downgrade or signature checks fail, indicating integrity issues on industrial sessions. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
